title: Can you shorten the prayer (qasr) when traveling for pleasure or vacation?
stanceSummary: By unanimous scholarly consensus, any permissible (mubah) travel qualifies for shortening prayer. No scholar has ever restricted qasr to business travel. Vacation trips qualify.
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2025 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g9qedAEBh8Y) (starting at [33:27](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g9qedAEBh8Y&t=2007s)), Yasir Qadhi addressed whether prayer can be shortened during leisure or vacation travel:

- By **unanimous consensus** of all the scholars of Islam, any **safar** (travel) that is **mubah** (permissible) qualifies for shortening the prayer.
- **No scholar** has ever said that only business travel qualifies for qasr.
- Even if you are going on a **family vacation**, you may do qasr of the salah.

---

## Full Lecture Transcript (Cleaned)

### The Question — [33:27](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g9qedAEBh8Y&t=2007s)

Can the shortening of the salah be done on traveling that is not for business — i.e., for pleasure and vacationing? If I take a vacation, can I shorten the salah?

### The Ruling — [33:41](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g9qedAEBh8Y&t=2021s)

**By unanimous consensus of all the scholars of Islam, the safar that you do that is mubah — you may shorten your salah.** No scholar said only business travel qualifies. Any permissible safar — so even if you're going on a family vacation, **you may do qasr of the salah.** No problem.
